title: Micron shares surge 9% and quadruple revenue driven by AI demand
summary: The chipmaker's quarterly results beat expectations, allaying fears about AI and boosting the global tech sector.

title: Apple raises MacBook and iPad prices by up to $200 due to rising memory costs
summary: The increase in memory chip costs, driven by AI demand, forces Apple to pass on the rise to its devices.

title: Oil falls below $70 to pre-Iran war levels
summary: Increased supply from the Persian Gulf and geopolitical easing bring crude back to pre-conflict levels.

title: SpaceX issues bonds with demand four times higher as its shares fall 31%
summary: Elon Musk's aerospace company taps the debt market to raise $25 billion amid a sharp market correction.

title: IBM presents the world's first sub-1 nanometer chip, the size of a fingernail
summary: The new processor represents a revolutionary breakthrough for AI and quantum computing, with greater energy efficiency.

title: US PCE inflation rises to 4.1%, highest in three years, keeping pressure on the Fed
summary: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge is in line with expectations but remains elevated, with a rate hike not ruled out.

title: Bitcoin falls to 20-month low on market pessimism and risk aversion
summary: The world's largest cryptocurrency plunges to levels not seen since October 2024 amid an adverse macro environment.

title: MiCA regulation causes Binance to exit Greece to seek license in another EU country
summary: The entry into force of the European cryptocurrency law forces exchanges to restructure and comply with new requirements.

title: Gold falls below $4,000 for the first time, weighed down by the dollar and interest rates
summary: The precious metal breaks the $4,000 support and is on track for its worst year in decades given the strong dollar and rate hikes.

title: Fed releases stress tests: biggest US banks could withstand $700 billion in losses
summary: The Federal Reserve results show banks have enough capital to withstand a severe economic recession.

title: Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, double previous plans
summary: The e-commerce giant reinforces its commitment to the Indian market after meeting with Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

title: Ibex 35 rises 0.44% and nears 19,500 points, boosted by banks and utilities
summary: The Spanish benchmark extends its winning streak to three sessions in a day without major macroeconomic references.

title: Merck KGaA buys a medical technology company for $11.3 billion
summary: The German pharmaceutical and chemical group strengthens its life sciences division with the largest acquisition in its history.

title: Bain Capital buys Volkswagen's marine engine unit for €7.4 billion
summary: The private equity firm acquires a majority stake in the German group's boat engine business, in a deal that strengthens its industrial presence.

title: EasyJet rejects £4.9 billion buyout offer and negotiates with Castlelake
summary: The low-cost airline dismisses the initial proposal but continues talks with the investment firm for a possible alliance.

title: Trump rules out enacting affordable housing law in the US
summary: The US president decides not to sign the bipartisan bill aimed at improving access to housing.

title: Two leading AI scientists leave Google to join Anthropic
summary: The talent exodus in AI continues at the tech giant, which loses two of its top researchers to the Amazon-backed startup.

title: British banks win legal battle against regulator over historic loans
summary: The High Court rules in favor of the institutions in a long-running dispute over the handling of past loan claims.

title: Largest IPO in China in four years is led by a renewable energy company
summary: A Chinese clean energy company raises billions of dollars in its stock market debut amid the green investment boom.

title: German neobank N26 turns profitable for the first time
summary: The online financial institution leaves losses behind and achieves profits thanks to revenue growth and cost discipline.
